canonical_input.raw_message: Interface GigabitEthernet0/0/2 on RTR-OSL-001 is down. Physical link loss detected. No carrier signal received.

perturbed_input.raw_message: Port GigE0/0/2 on RTR-OSL-001 has gone offline. Loss of physical connectivity confirmed. Carrier signal is absent.

expected.notes: Paraphrase replaces technical interface naming convention and rewrites status description using different but equivalent networking terminology. Classifier must recognise link failure regardless of interface label format.

canonical_input.raw_message: CPU utilization on RTR-OSL-010 has reached 94% over the last 5 minutes. Process scheduling delays observed. System performance degraded.

perturbed_input.raw_message: Processor load on RTR-OSL-010 is running at 94% capacity across the past five-minute interval. Delays in task scheduling have been noted. Overall device throughput is impaired.

expected.notes: Paraphrase substitutes CPU-specific vocabulary with processor/load terminology and rephrases the time window and impact description. Classifier must correlate high processor load language with HIGH_CPU alarm type.

canonical_input.raw_message: BGP session with peer 192.168.45.1 on RTR-OSL-015 has dropped. Hold timer expired. Prefixes from AS65002 are no longer being received.

perturbed_input.raw_message: The peering session established with neighbour 192.168.45.1 on RTR-OSL-015 has been torn down. The keepalive hold interval timed out. Route advertisements originating from autonomous system 65002 are no longer incoming.

expected.notes: Paraphrase avoids the term BGP entirely and uses alternative routing protocol vocabulary such as peering session, neighbour, and autonomous system. Classifier must infer BGP_SESSION_DOWN from contextual routing terminology without explicit BGP keyword.

canonical_input.raw_message: Packet loss of 18% detected on interface Ethernet1/3 of RTR-OSL-020. Consecutive dropped frames observed in outbound traffic stream. QoS thresholds exceeded.

perturbed_input.raw_message: An 18% drop rate has been measured on port Eth1/3 of RTR-OSL-020. Repeated frame discards are occurring within the egress traffic flow. Configured quality-of-service limits have been breached.

expected.notes: Paraphrase replaces packet loss with drop rate and frame discards, and substitutes outbound with egress and QoS with quality-of-service. Classifier must map alternative data-plane loss descriptors back to the PACKET_LOSS alarm type.

canonical_input.raw_message: BGP session to peer 10.0.0.1 has gone down on RTR-OSL-002. Neighbor relationship lost after hold timer expiry.

perturbed_input.raw_message: Peering connection with 10.0.0.1 terminated on RTR-OSL-002. The neighbor adjacency was dropped following hold timer expiration.

expected.notes: The paraphrase replaces 'session gone down' with 'peering connection terminated' and 'neighbor relationship lost' with 'neighbor adjacency dropped', which could confuse classifiers that rely on specific BGP terminology.

canonical_input.raw_message: Memory utilization on RTR-OSL-010 has exceeded the configured threshold of 85%. Current usage is at 91%, risking process instability.

perturbed_input.raw_message: RAM consumption on RTR-OSL-010 has surpassed the 85% limit set by policy. Presently at 91% capacity, increasing the risk of system process failures.

expected.notes: The paraphrase substitutes 'memory utilization exceeded threshold' with 'RAM consumption surpassed limit', and 'process instability' with 'system process failures', which may mislead classifiers toward a more generic hardware fault category.

canonical_input.raw_message: Packet loss of 18% detected on interface Ethernet1/2 of RTR-OSL-015. Continuous drops observed over the last 5 minutes affecting user traffic.

perturbed_input.raw_message: An 18% drop rate has been identified on port Ethernet1/2 of RTR-OSL-015. Persistent frame discards measured across the past 5 minutes are impacting end-user data flows.

expected.notes: The paraphrase replaces 'packet loss detected' with 'drop rate identified' and 'continuous drops' with 'persistent frame discards', which are semantically equivalent but use different networking vocabulary that may challenge keyword-based classifiers.

canonical_input.raw_message: Power supply unit PSU-2 on RTR-OSL-021 has failed. Device is now operating on a single redundant PSU. Immediate replacement recommended.

perturbed_input.raw_message: The secondary power module PSU-2 on RTR-OSL-021 is no longer functional. The router is sustaining operations via its sole remaining power unit. Urgent hardware intervention is advised.

expected.notes: The paraphrase replaces 'power supply unit has failed' with 'power module is no longer functional' and 'operating on a single redundant PSU' with 'sustaining operations via its sole remaining power unit', testing whether classifiers recognize power failure semantics without explicit failure keywords.

canonical_input.raw_message: BGP session to neighbor 10.0.0.1 has gone down on RTR-OSL-001. AS65001 peer unreachable, hold timer expired.

perturbed_input.raw_message: BGP sesison to nieghbor 10.0.0.1 has goen down on RTR-OSL-001. AS65001 peer unreachble, hold timer expried.

expected.notes: Typos in key terms 'session', 'neighbor', 'gone', 'unreachable', and 'expired' may challenge keyword-based classification but semantic context remains clear.

canonical_input.raw_message: Packet loss detected on interface GigabitEthernet0/0/2 at RTR-OSL-015. Loss rate is 18% over the last 5 minutes, threshold exceeded.

perturbed_input.raw_message: Pacekt loss deteced on interface GigabitEthernet0/0/2 at RTR-OSL-015. Loss rate is 18% voer the last 5 mintues, threshold exceeeded.

expected.notes: Swapped letters in 'Packet', 'detected', 'over', 'minutes', and double-letter error in 'exceeded' may reduce confidence but loss context is preserved.

canonical_input.raw_message: Memory utilization on RTR-OSL-020 has reached 87%. Warning threshold of 85% exceeded. Process heap allocation approaching limit.

perturbed_input.raw_message: Memoyr utilizaton on RTR-OSL-020 has recahed 87%. Warnning threshold of 85% exceeeded. Process heap allocaiton approaching limit.

expected.notes: Transposed letters in 'Memory', 'utilization', 'reached', and dropped/added letters in 'Warning' and 'allocation' test resilience to common keyboard slip typos.

canonical_input.raw_message: Power supply unit PSU-2 failure detected on RTR-OSL-021. Redundant power lost, device operating on single PSU. Immediate replacement required.

perturbed_input.raw_message: Pwoer supply unit PSU-2 fialure deteced on RTR-OSL-021. Redundnat power lost, device operatng on single PSU. Immediate replacement requried.

expected.notes: Transposition in 'Power', dropped letters in 'failure', 'detected', 'Redundant', 'operating', and 'required' simulate realistic fast-typing errors by an operator entering manual alarms.

canonical_input.raw_message: Interface GigabitEthernet0/0/3 experienced excessive CRC errors, error rate exceeded threshold of 10^-6, link stability compromised

perturbed_input.raw_message: Interfce GigabitEthernet0/0/3 experineced excesive CRC erorrs, error rate exceded threshold of 10^-6, lnik stability compromised

expected.notes: Multiple dropped and swapped letters across key terms like 'Interface', 'experienced', 'excessive', 'errors', 'exceeded', and 'link' may confuse token-level matching but semantic context remains clear

canonical_input.raw_message: Memory utilization on control plane reached 91 percent, free memory dropped below 512MB, process scheduler under pressure

perturbed_input.raw_message: Memoyr utilization on controll plane reachd 91 percent, free memory droped below 512MB, proccess scheudler under pressure

expected.notes: Typos in 'Memory', 'control', 'reached', 'dropped', 'process', and 'scheduler' introduce realistic operator keystroke errors that could degrade keyword-based classifiers while the numeric and structural context persists

canonical_input.raw_message: Power supply unit PSU-2 failed, redundancy lost, input voltage absent on secondary rail, immediate replacement required

perturbed_input.raw_message: Powre supply unit PSU-2 faield, redudancy lost, input voltge absent on sceondary rail, imediate replacement required

expected.notes: Typos in 'Power', 'failed', 'redundancy', 'voltage', 'secondary', and 'immediate' mirror common fast-typing errors; the PSU unit identifier and structural alarm pattern still anchor the classification

canonical_input.raw_message: Optical receive power on port Optical0/1/2 degraded to -24.5 dBm, approaching receiver sensitivity limit, signal quality marginal

perturbed_input.raw_message: Opitcal recieve power on port Optical0/1/2 degreded to -24.5 dBm, aproaching reciever sensitivty limit, singal quality marginal

expected.notes: Typos in 'Optical', 'receive', 'degraded', 'approaching', 'receiver', 'sensitivity', and 'signal' reflect common transposition and omission errors; the dBm value and port identifier preserve enough signal for correct classification

canonical_input.raw_message: Router RTR-OSL-001 reports high error rate on interface GigabitEthernet0/0/1 at Stockholm-DC-North. CRC errors exceeding threshold: 1500 errors/min. Link state remains UP but packet loss at 12%.

perturbed_input.raw_message: Device RTR-OSL-001 reports high error rate on ge-0/0/1 at Stockholm-DC-North. CRC errors exceeding threshold: 1500 errors/min. Adjacency state remains UP but packet loss at 12%.

expected.notes: Juniper-style interface notation 'ge-0/0/1' replaces 'GigabitEthernet0/0/1', 'device' replaces 'router', and 'adjacency' replaces 'link state'. Classifier must recognize these as equivalent interface error indicators.

canonical_input.raw_message: Router RTR-OSL-010 at Paris-DC-South has exceeded memory utilization threshold. Current memory usage is 94% of total available RAM. Process heap allocation failures detected. Immediate action required to prevent session drops.

perturbed_input.raw_message: Node RTR-OSL-010 at Paris-DC-South has exceeded memory utilization threshold. Current memory usage is 94% of total available RAM. Process heap allocation failures detected. Immediate action required to prevent adjacency drops.

expected.notes: Term 'node' replaces 'router' (common in Nokia/Ericsson NMS contexts), and 'adjacency drops' replaces 'session drops'. The underlying memory exhaustion condition is identical and classifier should not be misled by these synonyms.

canonical_input.raw_message: Router RTR-OSL-020 at Frankfurt-DC-Central detects optical signal degradation on interface TenGigabitEthernet0/1/2. Receive power level dropped to -28.5 dBm, below minimum threshold of -22 dBm. Link remains operational but BER increasing.

perturbed_input.raw_message: Device RTR-OSL-020 at Frankfurt-DC-Central detects optical signal degradation on port Te0/1/2. Rx power level dropped to -28.5 dBm, below minimum threshold of -22 dBm. Interface remains operational but BER increasing.

expected.notes: Cisco shorthand 'Te0/1/2' replaces 'TenGigabitEthernet0/1/2', 'Rx' replaces 'Receive', 'port' replaces 'interface', and 'device' replaces 'router'. Optical power degradation signal is preserved and classifier must identify it across naming conventions.

canonical_input.raw_message: Router RTR-OSL-021 at Amsterdam-DC-West reports fan failure in chassis slot 2. Fan tray unit FAN-TRAY-2 has stopped rotating. Redundant cooling lost. Internal chassis temperature rising to 68°C. Hardware shutdown imminent if not resolved.

perturbed_input.raw_message: Network element RTR-OSL-021 at Amsterdam-DC-West reports cooling module failure in shelf position 2. Fan assembly FRU-FAN-2 has stopped rotating. Redundant airflow lost. Internal chassis temperature rising to 68°C. Hardware shutdown imminent if not resolved.

expected.notes: Telco-style term 'network element' replaces 'router', 'cooling module' replaces 'fan failure', 'FRU-FAN-2' (Field Replaceable Unit notation) replaces 'FAN-TRAY-2', 'shelf position' replaces 'chassis slot', and 'airflow' replaces 'cooling'. Classifier must associate cooling/airflow FRU failure with FAN_FAILURE category.

canonical_input.raw_message: Interface GigabitEthernet0/0/1 on router RTR-OSL-001 has gone down. BGP session to peer 10.0.0.2 is no longer established. Link failure detected on uplink port.

perturbed_input.raw_message: ge-0/0/1 on device RTR-OSL-001 is operationally down. BGP adjacency to neighbor 10.0.0.2 has been torn down. Physical interface failure detected on uplink.

expected.notes: Uses Juniper-style interface notation 'ge-0/0/1' instead of 'GigabitEthernet0/0/1', 'adjacency' instead of 'session', and 'device' instead of 'router'. System must recognize these as equivalent link failure indicators.

canonical_input.raw_message: Power supply unit PSU-2 on router RTR-OSL-010 has failed. Redundant power module is no longer providing output voltage. Immediate replacement required to maintain device operation.

perturbed_input.raw_message: PWR-2 fault detected on chassis RTR-OSL-010. Backup power inlet has lost DC feed. Immediate field replacement needed to sustain node operation.

expected.notes: Uses 'PWR-2' instead of 'PSU-2', 'chassis' instead of 'router', 'power inlet' instead of 'power module', and 'node' instead of 'device'. Vendor-specific power supply naming conventions may confuse classifiers.

canonical_input.raw_message: Spanning Tree Protocol topology change detected on router RTR-OSL-015. Interface FastEthernet1/0/3 transitioned to forwarding state. Root bridge re-election in progress on VLAN 100.

perturbed_input.raw_message: STP topology recalculation triggered on device RTR-OSL-015. Port fa1/0/3 moved to designated forwarding role. Root bridge reconvergence occurring on VLAN 100.

expected.notes: Uses Cisco shorthand 'fa1/0/3' instead of 'FastEthernet1/0/3', 'STP' acronym instead of full 'Spanning Tree Protocol', 'recalculation' instead of 'change', and 'reconvergence' instead of 're-election'. Abbreviated port naming may reduce confidence.

canonical_input.raw_message: Optical signal degradation detected on interface TenGigabitEthernet0/1/2 of router RTR-OSL-020. Received optical power has dropped below threshold at -28.5 dBm. Link performance is impaired.

perturbed_input.raw_message: Optic rx-power alarm raised on Te0/1/2 of node RTR-OSL-020. Ingress light level fallen below acceptable threshold at -28.5 dBm. Interface performance degraded.

expected.notes: Uses Cisco shorthand 'Te0/1/2' instead of 'TenGigabitEthernet0/1/2', 'rx-power' instead of 'optical power', 'ingress light level' instead of 'received optical power', and 'node' instead of 'router'. Telecom-specific optics terminology may challenge classification.

canonical_input.raw_message: RTR-OSL-001: Dual power supply failure detected. Both PSU-A and PSU-B have lost input feed. Device is operating on emergency UPS backup with estimated 8 minutes of runtime remaining. Immediate intervention required.

perturbed_input.raw_message: RTR-OSL-001: Dual power supply failure detected. Both PSU-A and PSU-B have lost input feed. Device is operating on emergency UPS backup with estimated 8 minutes of runtime remaining. Immediate intervention required.

expected.notes: Severity field is mislabeled as WARNING, but the raw message explicitly describes complete dual PSU failure with imminent device shutdown in under 10 minutes, clearly warranting CRITICAL classification.

canonical_input.raw_message: RTR-OSL-010: Core uplink interface GigabitEthernet0/0/1 is down. This interface carries all primary transit traffic between Frankfurt-DC-Central and Amsterdam-DC-West. Traffic is currently unrouted with no active failover path confirmed.

perturbed_input.raw_message: RTR-OSL-010: Core uplink interface GigabitEthernet0/0/1 is down. This interface carries all primary transit traffic between Frankfurt-DC-Central and Amsterdam-DC-West. Traffic is currently unrouted with no active failover path confirmed.

expected.notes: Severity is mislabeled as MINOR, but the message describes a core uplink failure carrying all primary transit traffic between two data centers with no failover in place, which clearly constitutes a MAJOR incident.

canonical_input.raw_message: RTR-OSL-015: Optical receive power on all four WDM channels has dropped below -28 dBm threshold simultaneously. BER has exceeded 1E-3 on all channels resulting in complete loss of coherent signal. All traffic on this fiber span is dropped.

perturbed_input.raw_message: RTR-OSL-015: Optical receive power on all four WDM channels has dropped below -28 dBm threshold simultaneously. BER has exceeded 1E-3 on all channels resulting in complete loss of coherent signal. All traffic on this fiber span is dropped.

expected.notes: Severity is mislabeled as MINOR, but the message describes total optical signal loss across all WDM channels causing complete traffic drop on the fiber span, which is unambiguously CRITICAL.

canonical_input.raw_message: RTR-OSL-021: All three fan tray modules have reported failure simultaneously. Chassis internal temperature has risen to 87 degrees Celsius and is climbing. Thermal shutdown threshold is 95 degrees Celsius. Device self-protection shutdown is imminent if cooling is not restored.

perturbed_input.raw_message: RTR-OSL-021: All three fan tray modules have reported failure simultaneously. Chassis internal temperature has risen to 87 degrees Celsius and is climbing. Thermal shutdown threshold is 95 degrees Celsius. Device self-protection shutdown is imminent if cooling is not restored.

expected.notes: Severity is mislabeled as WARNING, but the message describes total fan failure across all three trays with rapidly rising chassis temperature approaching thermal shutdown, clearly warranting at minimum MAJOR classification.

canonical_input.raw_message: LINK_DOWN: All uplink interfaces on RTR-OSL-001 are down. Device is completely isolated from core network. No redundant path available. Full service outage in progress at Stockholm-DC-North.

perturbed_input.raw_message: LINK_DOWN: All uplink interfaces on RTR-OSL-001 are down. Device is completely isolated from core network. No redundant path available. Full service outage in progress at Stockholm-DC-North.

expected.notes: Severity is labeled WARNING but message explicitly states complete device isolation, no redundant path, and full service outage — all hallmarks of a CRITICAL incident requiring immediate override.

canonical_input.raw_message: HIGH_CPU: RTR-OSL-010 CPU utilization sustained at 98% for 25 minutes. Control plane unresponsive. BGP session to upstream provider has dropped. Active traffic forwarding severely degraded at Frankfurt-DC-Central.

perturbed_input.raw_message: HIGH_CPU: RTR-OSL-010 CPU utilization sustained at 98% for 25 minutes. Control plane unresponsive. BGP session to upstream provider has dropped. Active traffic forwarding severely degraded at Frankfurt-DC-Central.

expected.notes: Severity labeled MINOR but message describes a 98% CPU condition with control plane failure, dropped BGP sessions, and severe traffic degradation — clearly a MAJOR event requiring severity override.

canonical_input.raw_message: OPTICAL_DEGRADATION: Total optical signal loss detected on RTR-OSL-020 trunk port Gi0/1. Rx power has dropped below minimum threshold (-40 dBm). All traffic on this fiber path has been lost. No protection switching activated. Amsterdam-DC-West is unreachable via this link.

perturbed_input.raw_message: OPTICAL_DEGRADATION: Total optical signal loss detected on RTR-OSL-020 trunk port Gi0/1. Rx power has dropped below minimum threshold (-40 dBm). All traffic on this fiber path has been lost. No protection switching activated. Amsterdam-DC-West is unreachable via this link.

expected.notes: Severity mislabeled as WARNING despite message indicating total optical signal loss, failed protection switching, and complete site unreachability — unambiguous CRITICAL condition that the agent must identify through content analysis.

canonical_input.raw_message: PACKET_LOSS: RTR-OSL-021 reporting 65% packet loss on core-facing interface Te0/0/2 for the past 18 minutes. Customer-affecting service degradation confirmed. SLA breach threshold exceeded on multiple enterprise circuits at Paris-DC-South.

perturbed_input.raw_message: PACKET_LOSS: RTR-OSL-021 reporting 65% packet loss on core-facing interface Te0/0/2 for the past 18 minutes. Customer-affecting service degradation confirmed. SLA breach threshold exceeded on multiple enterprise circuits at Paris-DC-South.

expected.notes: Severity filed as MINOR but 65% sustained packet loss with confirmed customer impact and SLA breach across multiple enterprise circuits is textbook MAJOR. Agent must override based on operational content of the message.

canonical_input.raw_message: Optical power level dropped below threshold on interface Gi0/0/1. Received power: -28.4 dBm, threshold: -25.0 dBm. Possible fiber degradation or connector issue on RTR-OSL-001.

perturbed_input.raw_message: SYSTEM: Auto-generated alert from NMS v4.2.1 [timestamp=1731551520] [node=NMS-01] ALERT_TEXT: Optical power level dropped below threshold on interface Gi0/0/1. Received power: -28.4 dBm, threshold: -25.0 dBm. Possible fiber degradation or connector issue on RTR-OSL-001. END_OF_ALERT [correlation_id=cx-88312] [suppressed=false]

expected.notes: The real alarm content is embedded between NMS boilerplate header and footer. The agent must isolate the ALERT_TEXT segment and not be confused by metadata fields like correlation_id and suppressed.

canonical_input.raw_message: CPU utilization on RTR-OSL-010 has exceeded critical threshold. Current usage: 97% sustained over 5 minutes. Routing processes and BGP keepalives may be impacted. Immediate intervention required.

perturbed_input.raw_message: SYSTEM: Auto-generated alert from NMS v4.2.1 [timestamp=1731551520] [node=NMS-04] ALERT_TEXT: CPU utilization on RTR-OSL-010 has exceeded critical threshold. Current usage: 97% sustained over 5 minutes. Routing processes and BGP keepalives may be impacted. Immediate intervention required. END_OF_ALERT [correlation_id=cx-44901] [suppressed=false]

expected.notes: The NMS wrapper includes node and timestamp metadata that could distract classification. The agent must recognize that the node=NMS-04 field refers to the NMS infrastructure, not the alarming device RTR-OSL-010, and extract the actual CPU alarm text correctly.

canonical_input.raw_message: Spanning tree topology change detected on RTR-OSL-020, VLAN 110. Port Gi1/0/4 transitioned to forwarding state. Root bridge re-election may cause brief traffic disruption.

perturbed_input.raw_message: SYSTEM: Auto-generated alert from NMS v4.2.1 [timestamp=1731551520] [node=NMS-02] ALERT_TEXT: Spanning tree topology change detected on RTR-OSL-020, VLAN 110. Port Gi1/0/4 transitioned to forwarding state. Root bridge re-election may cause brief traffic disruption. END_OF_ALERT [correlation_id=cx-77045] [suppressed=false]

expected.notes: The boilerplate wrapping is syntactically similar in structure to the alarm content, with key=value pairs that could blend with alarm metadata. The agent must parse out ALERT_TEXT content and correctly classify the STP event without being misled by suppressed=false or correlation fields.

canonical_input.raw_message: Elevated packet loss detected on RTR-OSL-015 uplink interface Te0/0/2. Loss rate: 4.2% over last 10-minute polling interval. Downstream services may experience intermittent latency.

perturbed_input.raw_message: SYSTEM: Auto-generated alert from NMS v4.2.1 [timestamp=1731551520] [node=NMS-03] ALERT_TEXT: Elevated packet loss detected on RTR-OSL-015 uplink interface Te0/0/2. Loss rate: 4.2% over last 10-minute polling interval. Downstream services may experience intermittent latency. END_OF_ALERT [correlation_id=cx-55678] [suppressed=false]

expected.notes: The NMS boilerplate introduces a timestamp field in epoch format alongside the ISO timestamp in the structured data, which could confuse temporal parsing. The agent must strip the wrapper and focus on the ALERT_TEXT describing the packet loss condition rather than treating NMS metadata as part of the alarm content.

canonical_input.raw_message: LINK_DOWN detected on RTR-OSL-001 interface GigabitEthernet0/1. Physical layer connectivity lost. Upstream provider link to Stockholm-DC-North backbone is down. Immediate intervention required.

perturbed_input.raw_message: SYSTEM: Auto-generated alert from NMS v4.2.1 [timestamp=2024-11-14T03:22:00Z] [node=NMS-01] ALERT_TEXT: LINK_DOWN detected on RTR-OSL-001 interface GigabitEthernet0/1. Physical layer connectivity lost. Upstream provider link to Stockholm-DC-North backbone is down. Immediate intervention required. END_OF_ALERT [correlation_id=crl-88241a] [suppressed=false]

expected.correct_classification: LINK_DOWN on RTR-OSL-001 at Stockholm-DC-North — CRITICAL
expected.notes: The real alarm content is embedded between NMS boilerplate headers and footers. The agent must strip correlation metadata, system headers, and the END_OF_ALERT footer to isolate the true alarm message.

canonical_input.raw_message: HIGH_CPU utilization on RTR-OSL-010 at Frankfurt-DC-Central. CPU load has sustained above 92% for the last 10 minutes. Likely cause: routing table recalculation following topology change. Performance degradation expected.

perturbed_input.raw_message: SYSTEM: Auto-generated alert from NMS v4.2.1 [timestamp=2024-11-14T03:22:00Z] [node=NMS-04] ALERT_TEXT: HIGH_CPU utilization on RTR-OSL-010 at Frankfurt-DC-Central. CPU load has sustained above 92% for the last 10 minutes. Likely cause: routing table recalculation following topology change. Performance degradation expected. END_OF_ALERT [correlation_id=crl-19034f] [suppressed=false]

expected.correct_classification: HIGH_CPU on RTR-OSL-010 at Frankfurt-DC-Central — MAJOR
expected.notes: NMS wrapper introduces node identifiers and correlation IDs that could be mistaken for device or incident metadata. The agent must identify RTR-OSL-010 as the affected device rather than NMS-04 listed in the system header.

canonical_input.raw_message: BGP_SESSION_DOWN on RTR-OSL-020. Peer 192.168.45.2 (AS65200) session has dropped. Hold timer expired. Amsterdam-DC-West inter-AS routing is affected. Failover to secondary peer not confirmed.

perturbed_input.raw_message: SYSTEM: Auto-generated alert from NMS v4.2.1 [timestamp=2024-11-14T03:22:00Z] [node=NMS-07] ALERT_TEXT: BGP_SESSION_DOWN on RTR-OSL-020. Peer 192.168.45.2 (AS65200) session has dropped. Hold timer expired. Amsterdam-DC-West inter-AS routing is affected. Failover to secondary peer not confirmed. END_OF_ALERT [correlation_id=crl-55729c] [suppressed=false]

expected.correct_classification: BGP_SESSION_DOWN on RTR-OSL-020 at Amsterdam-DC-West — CRITICAL
expected.notes: The boilerplate contains a timestamp and node field (NMS-07) that could distract from the actual device (RTR-OSL-020) and BGP peer details. The agent must correctly extract peer IP and AS number from within the ALERT_TEXT block.

canonical_input.raw_message: PACKET_LOSS detected on RTR-OSL-015 at Paris-DC-South. Interface FastEthernet2/0 reporting 8% packet loss over 5-minute polling interval. Threshold exceeded. Possible congestion or partial physical fault.

perturbed_input.raw_message: SYSTEM: Auto-generated alert from NMS v4.2.1 [timestamp=2024-11-14T03:22:00Z] [node=NMS-03] ALERT_TEXT: PACKET_LOSS detected on RTR-OSL-015 at Paris-DC-South. Interface FastEthernet2/0 reporting 8% packet loss over 5-minute polling interval. Threshold exceeded. Possible congestion or partial physical fault. END_OF_ALERT [correlation_id=crl-30812e] [suppressed=false]

expected.correct_classification: PACKET_LOSS on RTR-OSL-015 at Paris-DC-South — WARNING
expected.notes: The suppressed=false flag and polling-interval language in the NMS wrapper may cause confusion about whether this is an active or suppressed event. The agent must correctly parse the ALERT_TEXT and not treat the suppressed field as indicative of alarm state.
